user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account VanArsdel Ltd. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 12:43 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at VanArsdel Ltd

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Patel <karen.patel@vanarsdelltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 12:16 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - VanArsdel Ltd
Date: 2024-03-09
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about timeline constraints. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:14:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy.

[00:16:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:18:00] Karen Patel (VanArsdel Ltd): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around.

signals.detail: Signal detected for VanArsdel Ltd: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-S trap (Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-S failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-S failure mode (Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-S -- Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
